frame_analyses.description: At 15 seconds, both instruments in field actively engaged near drain. Left instrument (serrated jaws visible) and right instrument working with needle and suture. Fast entry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instrument engagement comparable to PostV7 — fast, confident entry.

frame_analyses.description: At 37 seconds, both instruments working closely together near the drain. Suture visible through tissue. Coordinated needle driving in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth suture placement approaching completion. Instruments working efficiently and close together — hallmark of improved technique.

frame_analyses.description: At 59 seconds, suture loop visible near drain surface between instruments. Both instruments engaged. First throw wrapping in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Reaching the first throw by ~55 seconds — matches PostV7 pace and is the second-fastest in the series. Controlled wrapping visible.

frame_analyses.description: At 96 seconds, instruments near drain with suture. Knot being formed/tightened. Drain slit edges visible — beginning to close.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Late throw phase. All three throws likely completed or completing by ~100 seconds. Consistent with PostV7 pacing.

frame_analyses.description: At 126 seconds, both instruments near the knot site. Left instrument holding suture, right instrument positioned for cutting. Suture cutting in progress.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting phase. Instruments engaged at the knot — cutting suture tails.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame at 148 seconds. Instruments fully withdrawn. Completed knot clearly visible on Penrose drain — dark suture thread forming a compact X pattern. The drain slit shows GOOD CLOSURE — edges well-approximated by the knot. Short suture tails. Drain remains securely on suture block (not avulsed).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Excellent result matching PostV7 quality. Compact knot, good closure, short tails. This confirms the trainee can consistently perform at this level when not fatigued. The 148s completion time essentially matches PostV7's 147s.

knot_assessments.notes: Throw wrapping visible at frame 9. Pattern consistent with surgeon's knot. Confidence moderate.
Second throw completed between frames 9-14. Confidence low on hand determination.
Third throw completed. Final result shows excellent closure confirming secure knot construct. Confidence moderate-high based on final result.

technique_summary: This ninth video confirms the trainee's true skill level when rested — at 143 seconds with complete slit closure, it essentially matches PostV7 (142s/455.5). Two back-to-back sub-150 second performances with good quality proves the previous regressions (post-3 through postV8) were fatigue-driven, not technique loss. The trainee has achieved consistent, repeatable performance at 28% faster than baseline with maintained quality. Needle loading (10s), suture placement (33s), and throw speed (22s avg) all match or closely track PostV7 personal bests. The cutting phase at 28s remains the primary area for further time savings.
improvement_suggestions: Suture cutting at 28 seconds is now the biggest time sink — target 15 seconds to push total below 130s
Suture placement at 33 seconds is excellent but expert level is 20 seconds — continue refining
Practice sessions should be limited to 4-5 attempts to maintain this quality level
Consider this level (143-147s with complete closure) as the current reliable performance benchmark
strengths: CONFIRMED REPEATABLE PERFORMANCE — two sub-150s attempts (V7: 142s, V9: 143s) proves consistency
Complete slit closure maintained at speed — no quality trade-off
Needle loading locked in at 10 seconds — expert-approaching efficiency
All three throws completed correctly with apparent hand switching
Drain not avulsed — consistent tissue handling across all 9 attempts
Recovery from fatigue-driven regression (V8: 208s → V9: 143s) confirms skill retention

key_observations: Matches PostV7 within 1 second (143s vs 142s) — reproducible peak
65-second recovery from PostV8 fatigue dip — skill fully retained
28% faster than pre-training baseline — significant learning achieved
Two data points at sub-150s confirms this is the trainee's true current ability
The V3→V6→V8 regressions were fatigue, not skill loss
Recommendation: benchmark future sessions against 143-147s / 454-456 FLS
